title: sorbus americana var. rufoferruginea
intro: Sorbus americana var. rufoferruginea, also known as American Whitebeam, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the Appalachian Mountains in eastern North America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, leaves, and roots, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. The bark of Sorbus americana var. rufoferruginea contains tannins and other compounds that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, can be made from the bark, leaves, and roots of this plant, and are often used to support digestive health, reduce stress, and promote overall well-being.
